source_jobs.title: Human Resources Business Partner
source_jobs.description:
Strong location preference for Denver, CO or New York, NY.



LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV and cross-screen advertising. We deliver innovative advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today’s evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re looking for a proactive, people-focused HR Business Partner who thrives in dynamic environments and enjoys being close to the business. In this role, you’ll partner with managers and employees across assigned teams to support everything from coaching and employee experience to organizational planning and talent development.

You’ll work closely with the Senior HR Business Partner and collaborate across the HR team to help scale thoughtful HR practices that support both business goals and employee growth.

This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys balancing hands-on partnership with broader people initiatives — and who wants to continue growing as a strategic HRBP in a high-growth, evolving company.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build trusted relationships with managers and employees across assigned business functions

 - Coach leaders on performance management, employee development, team dynamics, feedback, and navigating challenging conversations

 - Support employee relations matters with professionalism, empathy, sound judgment, and discretion

 - Partner with HR leadership and business leaders to support organizational effectiveness, engagement, retention, and team health

 - Help drive key people processes, including performance reviews, talent planning, promotions, compensation cycles, engagement initiatives, and development planning

 - Use employee feedback, engagement data, exit interviews, and business insights to identify trends and recommend practical improvements

 - Support managers through organizational changes, team growth, and workforce planning discussions

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with recruiting, HR operations, leadership, and other HR partners to deliver a consistent and positive employee experience

 - Help improve and simplify HR tools, resources, and processes to make support more scalable and manager-friendly

 - Contribute to building an inclusive, high-performing culture where employees feel supported, challenged, and empowered to grow


REQUIREMENTS

 - 4+ years of progressive HR experience in an HRBP, People Partner or similar role

 - Experience supporting employee relations, performance management, manager coaching, and core people programs

 - Strong relationship-building, communication, and influencing skills across all levels of an organization

 - Sound judgment, discretion, and the ability to navigate sensitive situations thoughtfully and professionally

 - Comfortable using employee feedback, people data, and business context to identify trends and recommend improvements

 - Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution in a fast-paced, evolving environment

 - A collaborative, solutions-oriented mindset with a passion for improving employee experience and helping teams thrive

 - Experience in technology, media, advertising, or other high-growth environments preferred

 - Bachelor’s degree in HR, Business, Psychology, Communications, or a related field — or equivalent practical experience

LG Ad Solutions provides equal work opportunities to all team members and applicants, and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type on the basis of race, color, ethnicity, caste, religion, age, sex, including pregnancy, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by our policies or federal, state, or local laws.

We want to ensure that our hiring process is accessible. If you need reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process because of a medical condition or disability, please send an email to careers@lgads.tv to let us know the nature of your reque

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer II - Data Engineering and Platform
source_jobs.description:
LG AD SOLUTIONS

LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced developer to join our team in the role of Senior Software Engineer II – Data Engineering & Platform. This pivotal role focuses on providing technical vision and leadership for a major business component, managing end-to-end processes that operate at a high scale, processing several petabytes of data daily. The ideal candidate will have a robust background in data engineering, coupled with a proven track record in technical leadership. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to drive technical innovation and deliver robust, scalable solutions that meet our business needs.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Architect and Implement Scalable Data Solutions: Design, develop, and deploy comprehensive data solutions encompassing ETL pipelines, data warehousing, data architecture, data cataloguing, and data delivery mechanisms. Ensure these solutions are scalable, reliable, and maintainable to support evolving business needs.

 - Lead Data Platform Strategy: Develop and execute the strategic vision for the data platform, ensuring alignment with organizational objectives. Oversee the architecture and management of data systems to support efficient data storage, processing, and utilization.

 - Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Work closely with product managers, engineering teams, data scientists, and other stakeholders to design and implement effective data integration and hand-off mechanisms. Facilitate seamless data flow and accessibility across various systems and teams.

 - Optimize Performance and Scalability: Identify and address performance bottlenecks within the data platform. Implement strategies to enhance system performance, scalability, and reliability, ensuring the infrastructure can handle large-scale data processing efficiently.

 - Oversee Data Governance and Quality: Implement and maintain data governance frameworks to ensure data quality, accuracy, consistency, and security. Establish policies and procedures for data management, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements and industry best practices.

 - Mentor and Lead Engineering Teams: Provide technical leadership and mentorship to data engineers and other team members. Foster a culture of innovation, ownership, and continuous learning within the team, guiding them through complex technical challenges and project deliveries.

 - Conduct Code and Architecture Reviews: Lead code and architecture reviews to maintain high standards of code quality and system design. Provide constructive feedback and ensure adherence to best practices and organizational standards.

 - Stay Abreast of Industry Trends: Keep up-to-date with emerging technologies, tools, and methodologies in data engineering and platform development. Evaluate and recommend new solutions to enhance the data platform's capabilities and efficiency.




REQUIREMENTS:

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

 - Over 5 years of software development experience, with at least 2 years in a technical leadership role overseeing data engineering or data platform teams.

 - Strong proficiency in big data technologies, including Apache Spark/PySpark, Apache Airflow and Apache Kafka.

 - Experience with programming languages such as Scala and Python for developing robust data pipelines.

 - In-depth knowledge of both relational and non-relational databases, with the ability to design and optimize complex queries.

 - Hands-on experience with data visualization tools and techniques to present data insights effectively.

 - Proficiency in developing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines using tools like Jenkins and GitHub Actions.

 - Experience with Infrastruc

source_jobs.title: Sr. Platform & Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We require people to be on-site, 4 days/week at our Denver or NYC office and are unable to offer relocation support.




LG AD SOLUTIONS

LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We're moving to a hybrid infrastructure model. Our team has deep AWS expertise; what we're building now is the on-premises layer — bare metal compute, network fabric, storage, and the tooling to manage it all as code alongside our cloud footprint. This role is for someone who has lived in data centers and cloud environments equally, understands the tradeoffs deeply, and can help us architect something that's genuinely best-of-both.

You'll be a key technical voice in decisions about what lives on-prem versus cloud, how we bridge the two, and how we keep developers moving fast regardless of where their workloads run.

A note on what we're not looking for: If your entire career has been cloud-native, this probably isn't the right fit. We're building something that bridges two worlds, and we need someone who genuinely enjoys the physical infrastructure side — not just tolerates it.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Hybrid Infrastructure Engineering

 - Design, deploy, and maintain on-premises compute, storage, and networking infrastructure alongside our AWS environment

 - Own bare metal provisioning and lifecycle management — from rack and stack through OS deployment, patching, and decommission

 - Build and manage the network fabric connecting on-prem and cloud: VLANs, VPNs, BGP routing, firewall policy

 - Architect and maintain storage systems (SAN/NAS) and evaluate tradeoffs between on-prem storage and cloud-native alternatives

Platform & Automation

 - Extend our Infrastructure as Code practices to cover on-prem resources using Terraform and Pulumi alongside AWS tooling

 - Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines that work seamlessly across hybrid environments

 - Manage container orchestration (Kubernetes) across both bare metal and EKS — including cluster provisioning, networking, and storage across environments

 - Automate provisioning, configuration management, and drift detection at scale

Security & Compliance

 - Implement consistent security controls across on-prem and cloud: network segmentation, identity management, and secrets management

 - Contribute to audit and SOC 2 compliance efforts

 - Maintain compliance with enterprise security standards across the full stack

Monitoring & Incident Response

 - Build unified observability across hybrid environments — metrics, logs, and traces from on-prem and cloud in a single pane of glass

 - Participate in on-call rotation and lead incident response for infrastructure-layer issues regardless of where they originate

 - Define and maintain SLOs for platform reliability across the hybrid footprint

Collaboration & Documentation

 - Partner with engineering teams to help them understand the tradeoffs between on-prem and cloud for their workloads

 - Maintain thorough runbooks, architecture diagrams, and operational documentation

 - Contribute to infrastructure cost optimization across both environments




REQUIREMENTS

 - 7+ years of experience in Platform Engineering, DevOps, or Infrastructure roles

 - Demonstrated hands-on experience managing physical server infrastructure — bare metal provisioning, data center operations, and hardware lifecycle

 - Strong Linux systems administration: networking stack, storage management, kernel configuration, performance tuning

 - Solid networking fundamentals: TCP/IP, DNS, VLANs, firewalls, load balancers

 - Proficiency with Infrastructure as Code across both on-prem and cloud using Terraform and Pulumi

 - Experience running Kubernetes on bare metal as well as managed cloud (EKS)

 - Hands-on AWS experience: EC2, EKS, VPC networking, IAM, RDS, and rela

source_jobs.title: Associate Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
THIS IS AN ENTRY LEVEL POSITION

Hybrid: 2 days a week in Sao Paulo office

 

LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.

 


THE OPPORTUNITY

The Associate Account Manager supports the growth and success of client accounts by partnering closely with Account Managers, Sales, Operations, and Product teams. This role is responsible for helping execute campaigns, maintaining strong client relationships, monitoring performance, and ensuring seamless post-sale support.

The ideal candidate is organized, detail-oriented, eager to learn, and passionate about digital media. This position provides an opportunity to develop expertise in account management, campaign strategy, client services, and the rapidly growing CTV advertising industry.

 


RESPONSIBILITIES

● Support Account Managers in managing day-to-day client relationships and campaign execution.

● Develop and manage media plans, including audience targeting, channel selection, budget allocation, flighting schedules, and performance optimization to achieve campaign objectives.

● Assist with campaign launches, reporting, optimization tracking, and delivery pacing to ensure client objectives are met.

● Monitor campaign performance and identify opportunities for optimization and incremental growth.

● Coordinate with Sales, Operations, Product, and other internal teams to ensure successful campaign delivery and client satisfaction.

● Prepare client-facing reports, presentations, and performance recaps with clear insights and recommendations.

● Help troubleshoot campaign issues and facilitate timely resolution with internal stakeholders.

● Maintain accurate account records, project timelines, and updates within internal systems.

● Develop an understanding of client goals, KPIs, and business objectives to support strategic recommendations.

● Stay informed on LG Ad Solutions’ products, capabilities, and industry trends to effectively support clients and internal teams.

● Contribute ideas to improve processes, workflows, and overall client experience.

 


REQUIREMENTS

● 0–2 years of experience in account management, client services, digital advertising, media, marketing, or a related field.

● Bachelor's degree or equivalent professional experience preferred.

● Interest in digital media, advertising technology, and the connected TV ecosystem.

● Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines.

● Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

● Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and identify trends.

● Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and familiarity with business tools such as Salesforce, Asana, or similar platforms is a plus.

● Detail-oriented with strong problem-solving skills and a proactive attitude.

● Ability to work collaboratively across teams in a fast-paced environment.

● Eagerness to learn, grow professionally, and take on increasing responsibility over time.

● This is a hybrid role. 2-3 days a week are required in the London office (Holborn area)

 


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

● Internship or prior experience in media, advertising, marketing, client services, or account support.

● Familiarity with digital advertising concepts, campaign metrics, and reporting.

● Exposure to CTV, programmatic advertising, or ad technology is a plus.

LG Ad Solutions provides equal work opportunities to all team members and applicants and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type on the basis of race, color, ethnicity, caste, religion, age, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by our policies or federal, state, or local laws.

We want to ensure that our hiring pro

source_jobs.title: Backend Software Engineer (C++, Go)
source_jobs.description:
We require people to be on-site, 4 days/week at our Manhattan or Denver office and are unable to offer relocation support.




LG AD SOLUTIONS

LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

We are seeking someone to work on our Automatic Content Recognition (ACR) platform. This platform consists of a client-side SDK embedded in smart TVs, a back-end engine to conduct content matching against a large content reference database, and a series of algorithms. Our platform collects and processes billions of pieces of data per day and is the backbone of our business.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Design, develop, and maintain scalable systems capable of low latency and high throughput using Golang and C++.

 - Collaborate on systems architecture updates.

 - Build data pipelines in Kafka.

 - Help drive best practices in QA, testing, and monitoring.





YOUR EXPERTISE:

 - 6+ years of backend engineering experience with an emphasis on low-latency, high-throughput systems that can scale efficiently

 - Code regularly in Golang and/or C++

 - Experience building high volume data pipelines with Kafka

 - Experience with debugging tools (e.g., GDB, Delve)

 - Experience working in an AWS environment that embraces IaC

 - Proficient in writing highly performant concurrent code

 - Writing clear technical documentation

LG Ad Solutions provides equal work opportunities to all team members and applicants, and it prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type on the basis of race, color, ethnicity, caste, religion, age, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by our policies or federal, state, or local laws.

We want to ensure that our hiring process is accessible. If you need reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process because of a medical condition or disability, please send an email to careers@lgads.tv to let us know the nature of your request.

Application Deadline: July 7, 2026

Compensation & Benefits

 - Tier 1* (San Francisco Bay Area, New York City Tri-State Area, Los Angeles Metro Area, and Seattle Metro Area)$154,000 - $220,000/year

 - Tier 2* (all other U.S. Locations outside of Tier 1)$135,000 – $192,500/yr

 - Offers Bonus

 - Benefits & Perks: 100% employer-paid medical, dental, and vision coverage for employees and eligible dependents. Company-paid life and AD&D, STD and LTD insurance, plus optional supplemental coverage. 401(k) with company match. Flexible Time Off (FTO). Paid parental leave.

*Certain markets, including the San Francisco Bay Area, New York City Tri-State Area, Los Angeles Metro Area, and Seattle Metro Area, are aligned to Tier 1 compensation bands. All other U.S. locations are aligned to Tier 2 compensation bands. The compensation offered will take into account internal equity and may vary depending on the candidate's geographic region, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

As a Product Manager at LG Ad Solutions, you will play a pivotal role in defining and driving the strategy, roadmap, and execution of our Ad Tech products. You will identify business needs from a growing portfolio of global customers and internal users and deliver solutions that enhance operational efficiency, improve customer satisfaction, and unlock revenue growth.

You will lead the end-to-end lifecycle of new features and products within our Ad Tech product suite - from ideation and development to launch and adoption - while collaborating closely with Engineering, Sales, Account Management, Product Marketing, and Operations teams across the US and India.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of strategy, technology, and execution - and who is passionate about building scalable, innovative advertising solutions that shape the future of TV and digital advertising.




RESPONSIBILITIES:




PRODUCT VISION & STRATEGY

● Define and execute a clear and innovative product vision and roadmap that elevates our AdTech and campaign management tools.

● Translate business and customer needs into high-impact product initiatives that drive measurable outcomes.

● Define and execute new product features to unlock additional revenue or gain efficiency in end to end campaign management.




CROSS-FUNCTIONAL LEADERSHIP

● Partner with Sales and Account Management leadership to identify opportunities to increase revenue and improve customer experience and efficiency when using our internal platforms.

● Collaborate with Engineering, Sales, Marketing, and Operations to deliver key product goals. Participate in strategy sessions, product planning, prioritization calls and lead sprint calls and go to market trainings.

● Collaborate with UI/UX teams to enhance user experience and ensure product usability, delivering carefully thought-out user flows.




STAKEHOLDER PARTNERSHIP

● Spend significant time working with internal teams to gather feedback, understand challenges, and define the product direction for all internal projects

● Act as the main point of contact between business and technical teams to ensure alignment and drive products forward

● Communicate roadmap updates, feature rollouts, and performance insights across internal stakeholders, organize training and release sessions




PRODUCT EXECUTION & DELIVERY EXCELLENCE

● Drive end-to-end product lifecycle - from planning through successful launch and adoption.

● Ensure product releases meet performance goals, scalability standards, and user satisfaction metrics. Monitor usage of the tools and platforms on an ongoing basis to identify usage patterns and opportunities for improvement

● Manage integrations across reporting systems, self-serve platforms, and finance/billing tools to ensure seamless workflows during the end to end management of media campaigns.

● Monitor and analyze product performance metrics to inform roadmap and optimization decisions, striving for high-performance campaigns and higher-.




REQUIREMENTS:

● Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

● 3+ years of experience in Product Management, ideally within AdTech, DSPs, or Advertising Campaign Management tools.

● Proven experience working with cross-functional teams and managing the delivery of complex product lifecycles.

● Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to convert insights into product strategy.

● Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

● Experience collaborating with engineering and design teams to deliver scalable solutions.

● Comfortable working across global time zones (US/India)




PREFERRED:

● Experience building

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer I (Fullstack)
source_jobs.description:
We require people to be on-site, 4 days/week at our Denver office and are unable to offer relocation support.




LG AD SOLUTIONS

LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

We are seeking a talented and motivated Senior Software Engineer 1 (Full Stack) to join our Demand Side Platform (DSP) team. DSP is a technology platform that enables advertisers and agencies to programmatically purchase and manage digital advertising inventory across multiple ad exchanges and supply sources.

Our DSP team operates at a massive scale, processing over 1.5 billion ad requests daily. In this role, you'll be at the forefront of building intelligent systems that make split-second decisions on ad delivery—determining the optimal ad to serve to each user, at precisely the right moment, at the right price point, and in the most effective format. Your work will directly power the real-time decisioning engine that connects premium advertisers with their target audiences across connected TV and digital platforms.

You will work on both frontend and backend development, building and maintaining the web applications and services that power our advertising platform. You will be responsible for developing user interfaces, APIs, and backend services, collaborating with cross-functional teams, and contributing to all stages of the development lifecycle. If you're passionate about building efficient, scalable, and user-friendly applications at scale in the adtech space, we want to hear from you!




RESPONSIBILITIES:

● Design, develop, deploy and maintain scalable web applications across both frontend and backend. This includes developing UI features, APIs, and database integrations.

● Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code while adhering to best practices across the full technology stack.

● Develop responsive and intuitive user interfaces while simultaneously building robust backend services and APIs.

● Design and implement backend services, RESTful APIs, and microservices architectures to support application functionality and business logic.

● Manage databases and ensure data integrity through effective backend development. Optimize database queries and design efficient data models.

● Work on micro services which are integrated with the UI application, ensuring seamless communication between frontend and backend systems.

● Identify and address performance bottlenecks. Optimize system performance, scalability, and reliability across both client and server-side components.

● Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues across both frontend and backend systems.

● Conduct code reviews, providing constructive feedback to ensure high code quality and adherence to best practices.

● Provide technical mentorship to junior engineers and foster a culture of innovation.




REQUIREMENTS:

● Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

● 3+ years of experience in full stack software development, with demonstrated ability to work effectively on both frontend and backend technologies.

● Proficiency in ReactJS/AngularJS/VueJS/TypeScript and NodeJS programming languages.

● Expertise in working with relational and NoSQL databases (e.g., MySQL, MongoDB, Redis). Experience with database design and optimization.

● Proficiency in writing and maintaining unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests.

● Demonstrated ability to stay current with AI tools and emerging technologies, with a quick learning aptitude for adopting new tools and frameworks.

● Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.

● Strong communication and collaboration abilities.




PREFERRED:

● Familiarity with Go (Golang) programming language.

● Experience in utilizing Apache Kafka as a message queue.

● Experi

source_jobs.title: Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

We are growing and looking to hire a highly motivated Security Analyst to our security team who will lead security analysis, incident response and threat hunting. You will be working with EDR, MDR, network security tools, SIEM, phishing simulations, email security, application security and help in compliance related tasks. We are looking for someone with a background in cloud environment who can help in improving security in AWS. This role will report directly to Senior Security Engineer II.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

● A successful candidate will thrive in a dynamic setting, leveraging their expertise in malicious attacks to identify and respond to cybersecurity incidents as a subject matter expert.

● Monitor, triage, and support incident response for suspicious activity, leveraging SIEM, EDR, MDR and other tools.

● Analyze system logs, alerts, and telemetry to detect threats and identify root causes of vulnerabilities and incidents.

● Collaborate on compliance and privacy tasks to facilitate responding to RFIs, RFPs and other audits.

● Lead vulnerability management efforts, including triage, prioritization, validation, and remediation in partnership with engineering teams.

● Partner closely with engineering, DevOps, and platform teams to drive secure design and implementation practices.

● Help in getting and maintaining security certifications such as SOC2. Implement NIST and CIS guidelines within LG Ads infrastructure.

● Stay up-to-date on changes to relevant cybersecurity regulations and standards, and communicate those changes to stakeholders.

● Review and support SAST/DAST findings—help engineering teams interpret issues, validate false positives, dismiss findings when appropriate, and guide remediation.

● Contribute to the development of metrics and reporting mechanisms to track supply chain cybersecurity performance.




REQUIREMENTS:

● Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or related field, or equivalent practical experience.

● 5+ years of experience in security engineering, vulnerability management, or security operations.

● 3+ years experience with SIEM platforms (e.g., Splunk, Sentinel) and EDR tools such as CrowdStrike or equivalent.

● Strong hands-on experience with Linux systems (hardening, troubleshooting, log analysis, scripting).

● Solid understanding of cloud security, especially AWS (IAM, VPC, EC2, S3, security groups, logging, etc.).

● Familiarity with SAST/DAST tools and workflows, including triage, false-positive validation, and supporting developers.

● Strong understanding of cyber threat landscapes, attack vectors, and mitigation techniques.

● Strong understanding of common attack vectors, exploitation techniques, mitigation strategies and secure coding practices.

● Experience working with security frameworks such as NIST, CIS Controls, SOC 2, or ISO 27001.

● Proven ability to collaborate with engineering teams and drive remediation efforts to closure.

● Strong problem-solving, communication, and cross-functional collaboration skills.

● Willingness to participate in on-call rotation for security incidents.

Preferred:

● Hands-on experience with Crowdstrike (SIEM, EDR), AWS security services (e.g., GuardDuty, Security Hub, IAM Access Analyzer, Firewalls, etc.).

● Experience with infrastructure-as-code and policy-as-code (e.g., Terraform, CloudFormation), previous work with DevSecOps.

● Scripting/automation skills (Python, Bash, etc.) for remediation and security tooling.

● Experience with threat modeling, security architecture reviews and application security tools (SAST, DAST, etc.)

● Relevant certifications (e.g., GCIH, GCIA, GCLD, GCPN, AWS Security Specialty).

● Understanding of AI-driven or ML-based securi

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant & Workplace Operations
source_jobs.description:
LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

We are growing fast and looking for a friendly, detail-oriented, and highly motivated individual to fulfill a hybrid role: Workplace Experience Manager / Executive Assistant to manage our office in New York, NY and provide auxiliary support to our offices in Australia, and Chicago. This role will also provide administrative support to Senior Leadership team members.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

Executive Assistant

o Administrative support to SLT member as booking and managing travel, calendar management, building client relationships and completing expense reports.

o Implementing HR policies and company directives

o Coordinating and gathering stakeholder content for presentations and ensuring that content is organized and presentation ready

o One-off administrative projects

Office Management (Planning, Moves, Relocations, Expansions, and Downsizing Efforts)

o Develop detailed project plans and timelines for office moves, ensuring minimal disruption to business operations.

o Coordinate with internal teams (IT, HR, Operations, Marketing) and external vendors (moving companies, project managers, furniture designers, architects, contractors, etc.) to ensure all logistics are handled efficiently.

o Conduct site assessments to determine space requirements and layout needs.

o Manage the inventory of furniture and equipment, ensuring optimal use of resources and space.

o Oversee the physical setup of new office spaces, including workstation configuration, furniture placement, and equipment installation.

o Develop and manage the budget for office moves and workplace improvements.

o Negotiate contracts and manage relationships with vendors, including movers, furniture suppliers, and maintenance providers.

o Ensure all expenditures are tracked and reported accurately.

o Communicate move details and expectations clearly to all affected employees.

o Address and resolve any issues that arise during the move process promptly.

Workplace Operations

o Oversee the everyday operations of all offices including ordering supplies, snacks, maintaining necessary services

o Develop and implement strategies to improve the overall workplace experience for employees (quarterly office gatherings, monthly staff meetings, etc)

o Ensure the office environment is welcoming, functional, and conducive to productivity.

o Regularly assess employee satisfaction with the workplace and identify areas for improvement.

o Plan/organize internal and external programs/events and manage event logistics in collaboration with internal partners and external vendors to promote a positive work culture within a hybrid workplace.

o Ensure our offices stay compliant with local laws and regulations.

o Exercise strong business judgment and communication skills needed to interact with a variety of people and job functions.

o Assist Human Resources department with onboarding and offboarding tasks as they relate to relevant offices




REQUIREMENTS:

· Willing and able to come to our New York office 4 days per week (there is schedule flexibility in when you arrive and leave)

· 6+ years of office management experience

· Thrive in a fast-paced, multinational workplace

· Strong interpersonal skills, ability to communicate effectively in written form

· Self-starter and curious - motivated to create solutions and innovate in the workplace

· Extremely detailed oriented

· Inquisitive and solution oriented

· Working knowledge of Concur (or similar expense reporting tool)

· Effective cross-team collaborator

· [Preferred] Experience in tech sector, as well as in a service/assistant role

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer II, Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
LG AD SOLUTIONS

LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

We are looking for a highly driven Software Engineer 2 to join our Data & Platform Engineering team at LG Ad Solutions. In this role, your primary focus will be designing, building, and scaling robust, Terabyte-scale data pipelines that power our core advertising (DSP/SSP) and Automatic Content Recognition (ACR) systems. Beyond just moving data, you will be instrumental in developing essential Data Platform-related tooling, establishing rigorous Data Governance frameworks, and ensuring strict Data Compliance across our massive data ecosystem. This role is designed for an engineer who thrives on ownership, possesses strong computer science fundamentals, and is ready to tackle the complex distributed systems challenges inherent in managing high-velocity, high-volume data that directly impacts company revenue.


RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Core Data Engineering: Design, develop, test, and deploy robust backend services and data pipelines processing Terabytes of AdTech and ACR data.

 - Platform & Governance: Build and maintain internal platform tooling to enforce strict Data Governance, Data Quality, and Data Compliance standards across all data assets.

 - Task Definition & Scoping: Autonomously define the scope, technical enhancements, and deliverables for features of limited complexity, and provide accurate time estimates.

 - Feature Set Contribution: Effectively contribute to the planning and development of features affecting multiple components across our data ecosystem with minimal guidance.

 - Advanced Debugging: Independently troubleshoot, debug, and resolve somewhat complex bugs within distributed environments and massive-scale data processing workflows.

 - Technical Communication: Comprehend and clearly communicate complex technical concepts—including technical designs, system architecture, and key engineering decisions—to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 - Global Collaboration: Work closely with product managers, data scientists, and engineering counterparts in the US and India (requiring overlap with US time zones, typically aligning with core hours of 8 AM - 11 AM MST).


REQUIREMENTS:

 - Bachelor's Degree (B.Tech/B.E http://B.Tech/B.E.) in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.

 - 2+ years of relevant professional software development experience, with a strong preference for Backend or Data Engineering roles.

 - Strong proficiency in Data Structures and Algorithms.

 - Solid understanding of core Computer Science concepts, specifically Operating Systems, Computer Networks, and DBMS.

 - Exceptional problem-solving and analytical skills, with a proactive, self-starter attitude.

 - Clear and effective communication of technical challenges, both verbally and in writing.

 - Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively across time zones.

Preferred:

 - Experience with Big Data processing frameworks, specifically Apache Spark (PySpark or Scala-Spark).

 - Hands-on experience with modern data platforms like Databricks and orchestration tools like Apache Airflow.

 - Experience building, maintaining, and scaling complex ETL processes.

 - Familiarity with cloud computing platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and related infrastructure.

 - Understanding of distributed systems concepts and proficiency in Low-Level Design (LLD).

Why Join Us?

 - High Impact: Work on systems critical to the company's revenue stream—our team touches every single dollar this company makes.

 - Cutting-Edge Exposure: Gain hands-on experience with massive-scale data processing for advanced A

source_jobs.title: Sales Enablement Manager
source_jobs.description:
This is a TEMPORARY 9-month fixed-term contract.

Junior level role

LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

The Sales Enablement Manager will support the General Market EMEA Sales team across client communications, pipeline management, media planning, and responses to briefs.

This role offers exposure to the wider EMEA business, providing the opportunity to work with a diverse range of agencies and brands across multiple markets. For a high-performing individual, there is potential for the role to develop into a permanent position.

● Support the General Market Sales team in maintaining accurate and up-to-date Salesforce pipelines.

● Assist sellers with client engagement through email communications and participation in meetings.

● Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

● Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities.

● A proactive, motivated mindset with a strong desire to learn, contribute, and succeed in a fast-paced sales environment.

source_jobs.title: Director of Engineering, Platform & DevOps
source_jobs.description:
We require people to be on-site, 4 days/week at our Denver office and are unable to offer relocation support.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re seeking a Director of Engineering to lead our Platform & DevOps organization. You will define the platform strategy and oversee a large team delivering secure, reliable, cost-efficient, developer-friendly platforms: CI/CD, cloud infrastructure, Kubernetes/EKS, observability, and security-by-default. You’ll set multi-quarter roadmaps and OKRs, own budgets and vendor relationships, and partner closely with Product, Security, Data, and DevOps leadership to create paved roads that accelerate the entire engineering org.




RESPONSIBILITIES


ORG LEADERSHIP & TALENT

 - Build and lead a large team organization (tech leads and engineers); attract, develop, and retain top talent.

 - Define career ladders, succession plans, and healthy on-call practices; ensure sustainable delivery and team health.

 - Champion a culture of ownership, inclusion, and continuous improvement (blameless postmortems, strategic retention).
   


STRATEGY, PORTFOLIO & GOVERNANCE

 - Own the Platform & DevOps strategy and multi-quarter roadmap; align to company goals and product timelines.

 - Define portfolio governance: intake, prioritization, and trade-offs across security, reliability, developer experience, and cost.

 - Establish org-wide guardrails (golden paths, reference architectures, policy-as-code) enabling autonomy with compliance.
   


PLATFORM ARCHITECTURE & GOVERNANCE

 - Set technical direction for build/release, infrastructure automation, Kubernetes/EKS runtime, and observability.

 - Establish guardrails (golden paths, templates, policy-as-code, org standards) to enable autonomy with compliance.

 - Drive cost, performance, and availability improvements; own platform budgets/forecasts and FinOps practices.
   


DEVOPS & SECURITY

 - Standardize CI/CD (GitHub Actions/Jenkins or similar), artifact signing, progressive delivery.

 - Enforce security-by-design: SCA/SAST/DAST in pipelines, secrets management, least-privilege IAM, network segmentation, runtime hardening.

 - Lead remediation SLAs with Security; maintain audit-ready evidence for enterprise standards and regulations.
   


CLOUD INFRASTRUCTURE & RELIABILITY

 - Direct the design/operations of secure AWS platforms using IaC (Terraform/Pulumi) with drift detection and change controls.

 - Oversee EKS/Kubernetes multi-cluster/multi-region strategy, capacity planning, and resiliency patterns.

 - Define SLOs/error budgets and ensure effective incident response, post-incident learning, and reliability investments.
   


COMMUNICATION, DOCUMENTATION & CHANGE MANAGEMENT

 - Publish clear reference architectures, runbooks, and standards; keep documentation current and discoverable.

Communicate priorities, trade-offs, and status to execs and cross-functional partners; influence with data.

Requirements:

 - Bachelor’s in Computer Science, Information Systems, or equivalent practical experience

 - 12+ years total experience in Platform/SRE/DevOps/Infrastructure, including 5+ years leading tech leads or multiple teams.

 - Proven track record delivering secure, reliable, cross-team platforms serving many product squads.

 - Experience with SOC2 compliance.

 - Strong Linux, networking, and troubleshooting fundamentals.

 - Proficiency in Python/Bash; automation around CI/CD, infra, and operations.

 - Deep AWS experience (EKS/ECS/EC2/RDS/DocumentDB/DynamoDB, IAM, VPC, security groups) and Kubernetes in AWS.

 - IaC expertise (Terraform preferred; Pulumi a plus) with sound practices (modularity, versioning, policy checks, drift detection).

 - Observability at scale (logs/metrics/traces, alerting design, SLOs) and incident management/command skills.

 - Security leadership across build/deploy/runtime; RBAC/ABAC and identity standards (OAuth, SAML, OIDC); secrets management.

 - Executive communication; portfolio management; budget/FinOps ownership.

source_jobs.title: Platform & Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We require people to be on-site, 4 days/week at our Denver or NYC office and are unable to offer relocation support.




LG AD SOLUTIONS

LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We're growing our infrastructure team in Denver and looking for an engineer who has gotten their hands dirty with both physical infrastructure and cloud environments — someone who's moved past tutorials and has real scars from production systems. You don't need to have all the answers yet, but you should be able to own a task end-to-end, ask good questions, and learn fast in a hybrid environment that spans data centers and AWS.

You'll work alongside senior engineers on a team that's actively building out our on-premises layer — provisioning servers, managing network infrastructure, extending our IaC practices, and keeping everything running reliably. This is a role where you'll be doing real work from day one, with mentorship and context from engineers who've built these systems before.

A note on what we're not looking for:

This role isn't for someone who's only worked in fully managed cloud environments and wants to stay there. The on-prem side of this team is real and growing — if physical infrastructure sounds boring or intimidating, this probably isn't the right fit. If it sounds interesting and you want to learn it properly, we'd love to talk.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Hybrid Infrastructure

 - Assist with deployment and maintenance of on-premises compute, storage, and networking infrastructure alongside our AWS environment

 - Participate in bare metal server provisioning and OS deployment workflows

 - Support day-to-day network operations: troubleshoot connectivity issues, help maintain firewall rules, VLANs, and VPN configurations under senior guidance

 - Help maintain and document storage systems and cloud connectivity configurations

Automation & Platform Support

 - Write and maintain Infrastructure as Code using Terraform and Ansible for both on-prem and cloud resources

 - Support CI/CD pipeline maintenance and help onboard teams to existing platform tooling

 - Assist with Kubernetes cluster operations — deployments, troubleshooting, resource management — across bare metal and EKS environments

 - Contribute automation scripts and tooling improvements in Python or Bash

Security & Reliability

 - Follow established security standards for on-prem and cloud: help maintain RBAC configurations, apply patches, and support access management processes

 - Participate in on-call rotation with support from senior engineers; help investigate and resolve infrastructure incidents

 - Contribute to monitoring dashboards and alert configuration across hybrid environments

Collaboration

 - Work closely with senior engineers and application teams to understand infrastructure needs and translate them into tickets and tasks

 - Keep runbooks, wikis, and operational documentation current as you learn systems

 - Flag issues, ambiguities, and potential improvements — your observations from ground-level operations matter


REQUIREMENTS

 - 3–5 years of experience in infrastructure, DevOps, platform engineering, systems administration, or a related role

 - At least one full-time industry role beyond your first job (internships and co-ops count toward the foundation, but we're looking for professional experience in a production environment)

 - Comfortable with Linux system administration: file systems, networking, process management, bash scripting

 - Hands-on experience with at least one IaC tool — Terraform or Ansible preferred

 - Working knowledge of AWS fundamentals: EC2, VPC, IAM, S3, and basic networking concepts

 - Basic networking understanding: IP addressing, DNS, VLANs, firewalls — you don't need to configure BGP from scratch, but

source_jobs.title: Data Analytics Associate (Marketing Science)
source_jobs.description:
3-4-DAY MINIMUM ON-SITE ATTENDANCE EXPECTED IN NEW YORK, NY OFFICE.

LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.

Job Description:

We are building a Data Analytics and Operations team within Research to strengthen ownership of our analytics outputs and the readiness of our core data assets. This role focuses on ensuring that data is trustworthy, usable, and effectively translated into business- and client-ready insights.

You will work in ambiguous problem spaces, define standards where none exist, and take responsibility for turning complex data into reliable, consumable outputs. You will be comfortable navigating unknowns, following problems across systems, and adapting approaches as you learn more.

Responsibilities:

 - Work directly with large and complex datasets to investigate data behavior, quality, and downstream impact

 - Able to understand and follow analytics outputs, including acceptance criteria, documentation for consumption

 - Communicate findings, risks, and recommendations clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences

 - Help the team maintain workflows that support analytics delivery, including automation and AI-assisted tooling where appropriate

 - Partner closely with engineering, research, and product teams to surface issues and communicate resolutions

 - Identify gaps in data reliability or usability and take initiative to close them

 - Contribute to the development of an analytics discipline that prioritizes ownership, clarity, and impact over process overhead

 - Automate at scale – Replace manual, repetitive data processes with reliable, fast, and transparent workflows

 - Focuses on accuracy, clarity, and reliability of outputs

 - Executes well-defined analyses and investigations with guidance



Qualifications:

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to navigate ambiguity

 - 2+ years working experiences in SQL or Python, with comfort working directly with raw and intermediate datasets

 - Solid understanding of how data systems support business decisions and products

 - Strong sense of ownership and accountability for outcomes

 - You automate with purpose – You’ve applied automation and AI frameworks to business problems and deployed solutions that meaningfully improved accuracy, efficiency, and productivity

 - Ability to pivot approaches as new information emerges and unknowns are uncovered

 - Clear written and verbal communication skills

 - Bonus: Experience in ad tech, media, or data products

LG Ad Solutions provides equal work opportunities to all team members and applicants, and it prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type on the basis of race, color, ethnicity, caste, religion, age, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by our policies or federal, state, or local laws.

We want to ensure that our hiring process is accessible. If you need reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process because of a medical condition or disability, please send an email to careers@lgads.tv to let us know the nature of your request.

Application Deadline: July 7, 2026

Compensation & Benefits

 - Tier 1* (San Francisco Bay Area, New York City Tri-State Area, Los Angeles Metro Area, and Seattle Metro Area)$90,655 - $124,649/year

 - Offers Bonus

 - Benefits & Perks: 100% employer-paid medical, dental, and vision coverage for employees and eligible dependents. Company-paid life and AD&D, STD and LTD insurance, plus optional supplemental coverage. 401(k) with company match. Flexible Time Off (FTO). Paid parental leave.

*Certain markets, including the San Francisco Bay Area, New York City Tri-State Area,

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Measurement & Reporting
source_jobs.description:
WE REQUIRE PEOPLE TO BE ON-SITE, 4 DAYS/WEEK AT OUR MANHATTAN OR DENVER OFFICE AND ARE UNABLE TO OFFER RELOCATION SUPPORT.

LG AD SOLUTIONS

LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We are seeking a talented and motivated Senior Software Engineer I to join our Measurement and Reporting team. We enable our clients to visualize their impact across different verticals and empower our sales and operations teams to identify opportunities and address shortcomings during campaign execution.

You will primarily work on designing, building, and maintaining large-scale data platforms and pipelines that power our reporting, analytics, and AI-driven solutions. In addition, you will contribute to the development of agentic AI applications that help automate workflows, enhance operational efficiency, and unlock new capabilities across the organization. You may also occasionally work on frontend applications to deliver end-to-end solutions and improve user experiences.

If you're passionate about building scalable data systems, leveraging AI technologies, and solving complex engineering challenges at scale in the AdTech space, we want to hear from you!


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, develop, deploy, and maintain scalable data pipelines, ETL/ELT workflows, and data processing systems supporting reporting, analytics, and machine learning applications.

 - Build and optimize batch and real-time data processing solutions using modern data engineering frameworks and cloud-native technologies.

 - Design and maintain data models, data warehouses, and data lakes to ensure high-quality, reliable, and accessible data.

 - Develop and maintain integrations between internal and external systems, ensuring efficient data movement and consistency across platforms.

 - Build and enhance agentic AI applications, leveraging LLMs, AI agents, orchestration frameworks, and automation workflows to solve business problems.

 - Collaborate with product, analytics, operations, and engineering teams to identify opportunities for AI-driven automation and intelligent decision-making.

 - Contribute to backend services and APIs that support data products, reporting applications, and AI-powered solutions.

 - Occasionally develop frontend features and dashboards to support internal and customer-facing applications.

 - Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize data pipelines and AI systems for performance, reliability, scalability, and cost efficiency.

 - Implement data governance, quality checks, monitoring, and observability practices to ensure data integrity and operational excellence.

 - Conduct code reviews, provide constructive feedback, and promote engineering best practices across the team.

 - Mentor junior engineers and contribute to a culture of continuous learning, innovation, and technical excellence.


REQUIREMENTS

 - Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field.

 - 5+ years of experience in data engineering, software engineering, or a related technical role.

 - Strong experience building and maintaining large-scale data pipelines and distributed data processing systems.

 - Proficiency in Python and SQL, with experience developing production-grade data solutions.

 - Experience with modern data platforms and technologies such as Databricks, Spark, Kafka, Airflow, Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, or similar technologies.

 - Strong understanding of data modeling, data warehousing, and data lake architectures.

 - Experience working with relational and NoSQL databases (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis).

 - Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud.

 - Experience building APIs and backend services.

 - Familiarity with Generative AI technologies, AI agents, prom

source_jobs.title: Human Resources Business Partner
source_jobs.description:
Strong location preference for Denver, CO or New York, NY.



LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV and cross-screen advertising. We deliver innovative advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today’s evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re looking for a proactive, people-focused HR Business Partner who thrives in dynamic environments and enjoys being close to the business. In this role, you’ll partner with managers and employees across assigned teams to support everything from coaching and employee experience to organizational planning and talent development.

You’ll work closely with the Senior HR Business Partner and collaborate across the HR team to help scale thoughtful HR practices that support both business goals and employee growth.

This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys balancing hands-on partnership with broader people initiatives — and who wants to continue growing as a strategic HRBP in a high-growth, evolving company.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build trusted relationships with managers and employees across assigned business functions

 - Coach leaders on performance management, employee development, team dynamics, feedback, and navigating challenging conversations

 - Support employee relations matters with professionalism, empathy, sound judgment, and discretion

 - Partner with HR leadership and business leaders to support organizational effectiveness, engagement, retention, and team health

 - Help drive key people processes, including performance reviews, talent planning, promotions, compensation cycles, engagement initiatives, and development planning

 - Use employee feedback, engagement data, exit interviews, and business insights to identify trends and recommend practical improvements

 - Support managers through organizational changes, team growth, and workforce planning discussions

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with recruiting, HR operations, leadership, and other HR partners to deliver a consistent and positive employee experience

 - Help improve and simplify HR tools, resources, and processes to make support more scalable and manager-friendly

 - Contribute to building an inclusive, high-performing culture where employees feel supported, challenged, and empowered to grow


REQUIREMENTS

 - 4+ years of progressive HR experience in an HRBP, People Partner or similar role

 - Experience supporting employee relations, performance management, manager coaching, and core people programs

 - Strong relationship-building, communication, and influencing skills across all levels of an organization

 - Sound judgment, discretion, and the ability to navigate sensitive situations thoughtfully and professionally

 - Comfortable using employee feedback, people data, and business context to identify trends and recommend improvements

 - Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution in a fast-paced, evolving environment

 - A collaborative, solutions-oriented mindset with a passion for improving employee experience and helping teams thrive

 - Experience in technology, media, advertising, or other high-growth environments preferred

 - Bachelor’s degree in HR, Business, Psychology, Communications, or a related field — or equivalent practical experience

source_jobs.title: Product Manager (Ad Tech, CTV)
source_jobs.description:
LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

As a Product Manager at LG Ad Solutions, you will play a pivotal role in defining and driving the strategy, roadmap, and execution of our Ad Tech products. You will identify business needs from a growing portfolio of global customers and internal users and deliver solutions that enhance operational efficiency, improve customer satisfaction, and unlock revenue growth.

You will lead the end-to-end lifecycle of new features and products within our Ad Tech product suite - from ideation and development to launch and adoption - while collaborating closely with Engineering, Sales, Account Management, Product Marketing, and Operations teams across the US and India.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of strategy, technology, and execution - and who is passionate about building scalable, innovative advertising solutions that shape the future of TV and digital advertising.




RESPONSIBILITIES:




PRODUCT VISION & STRATEGY

● Define and execute a clear and innovative product vision and roadmap that elevates the TV OS product suite and audience management/media planning tools.

● Translate business and customer needs into high-impact product initiatives that drive measurable outcomes.

● Define and execute new product features to unlock additional revenue or gain efficiency in end to end campaign management.




CROSS-FUNCTIONAL LEADERSHIP

● Partner with Sales and Account Management leadership to identify opportunities to increase revenue and improve customer experience and efficiency when using our internal platforms.

● Collaborate with Engineering, Sales, Marketing, and Operations to deliver key product goals. Participate in strategy sessions, product planning, prioritization calls and lead sprint calls and go to market trainings.

● Collaborate with UI/UX teams to enhance user experience and ensure product usability, delivering carefully thought-out user flows.




STAKEHOLDER PARTNERSHIP

● Work with internal teams to gather feedback, understand challenges, and define the product direction for all internal projects

● Act as the main point of contact between business and technical teams to ensure alignment and drive products forward

● Communicate roadmap updates, feature rollouts, and performance insights across internal stakeholders, organize training and release sessions




PRODUCT EXECUTION & DELIVERY EXCELLENCE

● Drive end-to-end product lifecycle - from planning through successful launch and adoption.

● Ensure product releases meet performance goals, scalability standards, and user satisfaction metrics. Monitor usage of the tools and platforms on an ongoing basis to identify usage patterns and opportunities for improvement

● Manage integrations across DSP, reporting systems, and finance/billing tools to ensure seamless workflows during the end to end management of media campaigns.




REQUIREMENTS:

● Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

● 3+ years of experience in Product Management, ideally within AdTech, DSPs, or Advertising Campaign Management tools.

● Proven experience working with cross-functional teams and managing the delivery of complex product lifecycles.

● Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to convert insights into product strategy.

● Experience collaborating with engineering and design teams to deliver scalable solutions.

● Strong presentation skills with the ability to clearly communicate project roadmaps and status updates to executive and leadership teams.

● Comfortable working across global time zones (US/India/Korea)




PREFERRED:

● Experience with ad tech market research, supporting platform product launches, and involvement with

source_jobs.title: Senior Quality Assurance Engineer, Data & Platform Engineering
source_jobs.description:
We require people to be on-site, 4 days/week at our Denver or NYC office and are unable to offer relocation support.

LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We are looking for a Senior QA Engineer to be the quality leader embedded directly within our Data & Platform Engineering team. This team builds and owns terabyte-scale data pipelines, platform tooling, and data governance frameworks that sit at the core of our advertising technology. You will work shoulder-to-shoulder with data engineers, understand the complexity of distributed systems and large-scale ETL workflows, and own quality from design through production.

This is not a generic QA role. You will need to speak the language of data engineering—Apache Airflow, Spark, Databricks, cloud infrastructure—and bring a testing mindset that addresses the unique challenges of high-volume, high-velocity data systems. If you thrive on ambiguity, care deeply about quality at scale, and want your work to directly impact advertising revenue, this role is for you.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design and lead comprehensive test strategies for complex, ambiguous data pipeline and platform quality challenges, including ETL validation, data quality checks, and pipeline observability

 - Build scalable, maintainable test automation frameworks tailored to distributed data systems—covering unit, integration, and end-to-end testing of Spark jobs, Airflow DAGs, and backend services

 - Establish and own data quality gates within CI/CD pipelines, ensuring schema validation, data completeness, and consistency checks are embedded throughout the development lifecycle

 - Partner closely with Data Engineers, Platform Engineers, and the hiring manager to define the quality bar for new features and infrastructure changes

 - Create instrumentation and metrics to measure quality both pre-release and in production, including anomaly detection and alerting across our data ecosystem

 - Proactively identify architectural deficiencies affecting data quality and lead initiatives to address them

 - Drive parallelized test plan design to enable independent execution across a globally distributed team (US and India)

 - Mentor engineers on testing best practices specific to data systems—data mocking, test data management, pipeline idempotency testing, and more

 - Influence engineering decisions across team boundaries to continuously improve product quality and reduce defect escape rates




WHAT YOU BRING

 - 7+ years of QA engineering experience, with meaningful time spent testing data pipelines, backend services, or distributed systems

 - Proven ability to design and execute test plans for complex, ambiguous problem areas with limited guidance

 - Hands-on experience building extensible test automation frameworks from scratch, not just maintaining existing ones

 - Working knowledge of data engineering concepts: ETL/ELT patterns, pipeline orchestration, data quality dimensions (completeness, consistency, timeliness), schema validation

 - Demonstrated ability to define and implement quality metrics, simplify testing processes, and remove bottlenecks

 - Experience establishing quality gates in CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins, GitHub Actions, or similar)

 - Strong judgment on technical trade-offs between short-term needs and long-term quality architecture

 - Clear communicator who can convey testing strategy and quality risks to both technical and non-technical stakeholders

 - Experience mentoring engineers and improving overall team testing capabilities




NICE TO HAVE

 - Familiarity with Apache Airflow, Apache Spark (PySpark or Scala), or Databricks

 - Experience testing AdTech systems (DSP, SSP, ACR, or audience data platforms)

 - Knowledge of cloud infrastructure testing on AWS, GCP, or Azur

source_jobs.title: Product Manager (Ad Tech/CTV)
source_jobs.description:
LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

As a Product Manager at LG Ad Solutions, you will play a pivotal role in defining and driving the strategy, roadmap, and execution of our Ad Tech products. You will identify business needs from a growing portfolio of global customers and internal users and deliver solutions that enhance operational efficiency, improve customer satisfaction, and unlock revenue growth.

You will lead the end-to-end lifecycle of new features and products within our Ad Tech product suite - from ideation and development to launch and adoption - while collaborating closely with Engineering, Sales, Account Management, Product Marketing, and Operations teams across the US and India.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of strategy, technology, and execution - and who is passionate about building scalable, innovative advertising solutions that shape the future of TV and digital advertising.




RESPONSIBILITIES:




PRODUCT VISION & STRATEGY

● Define and execute a clear and innovative product vision and roadmap that elevates the TV OS product suite and audience management/media planning tools.

● Translate business and customer needs into high-impact product initiatives that drive measurable outcomes.

● Define and execute new product features to unlock additional revenue or gain efficiency in end to end campaign management.




CROSS-FUNCTIONAL LEADERSHIP

● Partner with Sales and Account Management leadership to identify opportunities to increase revenue and improve customer experience and efficiency when using our internal platforms.

● Collaborate with Engineering, Sales, Marketing, and Operations to deliver key product goals. Participate in strategy sessions, product planning, prioritization calls and lead sprint calls and go to market trainings.

● Collaborate with UI/UX teams to enhance user experience and ensure product usability, delivering carefully thought-out user flows.




STAKEHOLDER PARTNERSHIP

● Work with internal teams to gather feedback, understand challenges, and define the product direction for all internal projects

● Act as the main point of contact between business and technical teams to ensure alignment and drive products forward

● Communicate roadmap updates, feature rollouts, and performance insights across internal stakeholders, organize training and release sessions




PRODUCT EXECUTION & DELIVERY EXCELLENCE

● Drive end-to-end product lifecycle - from planning through successful launch and adoption.

● Ensure product releases meet performance goals, scalability standards, and user satisfaction metrics. Monitor usage of the tools and platforms on an ongoing basis to identify usage patterns and opportunities for improvement

● Manage integrations across DSP, reporting systems, and finance/billing tools to ensure seamless workflows during the end to end management of media campaigns.




REQUIREMENTS:

● Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

● 3+ years of experience in Product Management, ideally within AdTech, DSPs, or Advertising Campaign Management tools.

● Proven experience working with cross-functional teams and managing the delivery of complex product lifecycles.

● Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to convert insights into product strategy.

● Experience collaborating with engineering and design teams to deliver scalable solutions.

● Strong presentation skills with the ability to clearly communicate project roadmaps and status updates to executive and leadership teams.

● Comfortable working across global time zones (US/India/Korea)

● Experience with ad tech market research, supporting platform product launches, and involvement with

source_jobs.title: Technical Recruiter (Contract)
source_jobs.description:
Contract Length: 3 months (with potential 3-month extension)
Location: Bangalore, India
Working Model: Hybrid (Whitefield area), 10AM-7PM IST
Start Date: Immediate

 


LG AD SOLUTIONS

LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV (CTV) and cross-screen advertising. We pride ourselves on delivering state-of-the-art advertising solutions that integrate seamlessly with today's ever-evolving digital media landscape.

 


THE OPPORTUNITY:

We are seeking an experienced Technical Contract Recruiter to support our growing hiring needs. In this role, you will be responsible for full-cycle recruitment across a variety of technical and some non-technical roles. You’ll play a critical part in sourcing, engaging, and closing top-tier engineering talent, while partnering closely with hiring managers and internal stakeholders.

Our recruitment model includes a blend of in-house, full life cycle recruiting, and the use of staffing agencies during high-volume hiring periods. In the early phase of this role, your focus will be on screening employee referral candidates, confirming compensation expectations, and scheduling interviews for both referral and staffing agency candidates. You’ll also be involved in negotiating and extending offers to agency candidates and supporting agency submissions, including addressing inquiries from agency representatives.

This position requires a proactive, resourceful recruiter who understands technical requirements, can build strong candidate pipelines, and thrives in a fast-paced, high-growth environment. While based in Bangalore, some alignment with U.S. working hours will be necessary.

We use Ashby for applicant tracking and interview scheduling.

 


RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - In the first month, focus on:
   
   - Screening employee referral candidates
   
   - Confirming compensation expectations
   
   - Scheduling interviews via Ashby
   
   - Managing the offer process for all candidates

 - Manage the full recruitment lifecycle from sourcing and screening to interviewing and closing offers

 - Conduct intake meetings with hiring managers to align on role requirements and sourcing strategies

 - Build and maintain a robust pipeline of qualified technical candidates using LinkedIn, GitHub, professional networks, employee referrals, and other sourcing platforms

 - Conduct behavioral and technical screening interviews to evaluate candidate fit and qualifications

 - Ensure a top-notch candidate experience through clear, timely communication and follow-through

 - Maintain accurate and up-to-date records in our ATS (Ashby experience preferred)

 - Collaborate with U.S.-based team members and staffing agencies to align on hiring priorities and candidate progress

 - Provide weekly updates on pipeline activity

 


REQUIREMENTS:

 - Must live in or near Bangalore, India and be available to be in office periodically, 10AM-7PM IST

 - Minimum of 3-4 years of full-cycle recruiting experience, with at least 2 years focused on technical roles (e.g., software development, DevOps)

 - Proven track record recruiting for roles involving technologies such as C++, Golang, AWS, Frontend, and Backend development

 - Experience working with U.S.-based clients or overlapping with U.S. time zones is required

 - Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build effective relationships with candidates and internal stakeholders

 - Solid understanding of technical skill sets and current industry trends

 - Proficiency with ATS platforms (experience with Ashby is a plus) and sourcing tools such as LinkedIn Recruiter

 - Ability to work independently and manage multiple requisitions in a fast-paced environment

 - Experience in a startup or high-growth technology company is a strong advantage

notes: LG Ad Solutions is a global leader in connected TV and cross-screen advertising, with a mix of tech and non-tech roles.
